codeReview.summary: All three blocking issues from prior review are still present in the codebase. The code has not been fixed.

issues.description: Confidence calculation bug: uses sum of all pattern frequencies (`frequency_map.values().sum()`) instead of per-pattern frequency. Should be `*frequency_map.get(&final_pattern.name).unwrap_or(&1)` to correctly calculate confidence as pattern_frequency / total_observations.

Convention parsing broken: String slice index uses [3..] instead of [4..] for parsing '- **' prefix. For input '- **name**: description', position 0='-', 1=' ', 2='*', 3='*', 4='n'. The name starts at index 4, not 3. Should be `trimmed[4..colon_pos]`.

Anti-pattern retrieval unimplemented: `get_anti_patterns_for_error` is a stub that returns empty vec. The error_type parameter is unused (assigned to `_`), and the function always returns `Ok(Vec::new())`. This makes the anti-pattern retrieval functionality completely non-functional.

summary: FAIL - All three blocking issues from prior review remain unfixed. (1) Line 955: confidence calculation still uses sum of all frequencies instead of per-pattern frequency. (2) Line 1143: convention parsing still uses wrong string slice index [3..] instead of [4..]. (3) Line 1175: `get_anti_patterns_for_error` still returns empty vec - the stub was not implemented.
